title: Transform the Expression

content.problemStatement:
Transform the algebraic expression with brackets into RPN form. 



You can assume that for the test cases below only single letters will be used, brackets [] will not be used and each expression has only one RPN form (no expressions like a*b*c)



Input Format

The first line contains t, the number of test cases (less then 100).
Followed by t lines, containing an expression to be translated to RPN form, where the length of the expression is less then 400.


Output Format
The expressions in RPN form, one per line.
constraints: Expressions are fully parenthesized and contain only lowercase letters and the operators +, -, *, /, ^
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 100). Each of the next T lines contains a fully parenthesized algebraic expression consisting of lowercase letters and the operators +, -, *, /, ^. No spaces are present.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the corresponding expression in Reverse Polish Notation (postfix) on a separate line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The inner parentheses give b*c → bc*. Then a plus that result gives abc*+.
Compute a+b → ab+, compute z+x → zx+, then multiply the two results → ab+zx+*.
a+t → at+, b+(a+c) → bac++, c+d → cd+, exponentiation → bac++cd+^, finally multiplication with at+ → at+bac++cd+^*.

solution.approach: The expression is fully parenthesized, so the order of operations is dictated solely by the parentheses. We scan the string from left to right using a stack. Whenever we encounter an operand (a‑z) we output it immediately. '(' is pushed onto the stack. When we see an operator we also push it (its precedence is irrelevant because parentheses guarantee correct ordering). Upon encountering ')', we pop from the stack until the matching '(' is removed, outputting every popped operator. After processing the whole string, any remaining operators on the stack are appended to the output. This yields the unique RPN form.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the expression. Overall O(total length of all expressions).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) auxiliary stack space per test case.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args)throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String expr=br.readLine().trim();StringBuilder out=new StringBuilder();Deque<Character> stack=new ArrayDeque<>();for(char c:expr.toCharArray()){if(c>='a'&&c<='z'){out.append(c);}else if(c=='('){stack.push(c);}else if(c==')'){while(!stack.isEmpty()&&stack.peek()!='('){out.append(stack.pop());}if(!stack.isEmpty()&&stack.peek()=='(')stack.pop();}else{stack.push(c);}}while(!stack.isEmpty())out.append(stack.pop());sb.append(out).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # This function prints the input for hidden testcase i (0 <= i <= 20).
    # The actual expressions are handcrafted above; here we simply map i to the stored input.
    cases = [
        "1
a",
        "1
(b)",
        "1
((a))",
        "1
(a+b)",
        "1
(((a+b)*(c+d)))",
        "1
(((a+b)+(c+d))*(e+f))",
        "1
(((a+b)*(c+d))^((e+f)*(g+h)))",
        "1
(((a+b)*(c+d))*((e+f)*(g+h)))",
        "1
((a+a)*(a+a))",
        "1
((a+b)+(a+b))",
        "1
((a+b)*(c+d))",
        "1
((a-b)/(c-d))",
        "1
((a^b)^(c^d))",
        "1
(((a+b)-c)*d)",
        "1
((a/(b-c))+d)",
        "1
(((a+b)*(c-d))+(e/f))",
        "3
(a+(b*c))
((a+b)*(c+d))
((a+b)+(c+d))",
        "4
(a+b)
(a-b)
(a*b)
(a/b)",
        "2
((a+b)*(c+d))
((a^b)+(c^d))",
        "5
(a+(b*c))
((a+b)*(c+d))
((a+b)+(c+d))
((a^b)^(c^d))
((a+b)-(c-d))"
    ]
    print(cases[i])
